 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.4","title":"Hook output: top-line code + UX wording","description":"Place the allow-once code at the very top of the block output to avoid truncation.","notes":"Scope:
- Modify block output to prepend a single-line header: `ALLOW-24H CODE: abcd | run: dcg allow-once abcd`.
- Keep existing denial message intact below.
- Ensure no ANSI formatting breaks copy-paste.
- For non-TTY or JSON output, include code and full hash as structured fields (not buried in long strings).

Acceptance criteria:
- The code is always the first line of denial output.
- JSON output includes both code and full hash.
- No regression in existing denial formatting.

Verification:
- Add targeted unit tests for JSON fields and output ordering; run e2e harness to verify top-line code presence in logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.733222487-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:54:14.060885546-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T20:54:14.060885546-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented allow-once header line + JSON fields + tests; fmt/check blocked by pre-existing heredoc.rs parse errors","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.734647742-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:21.562962946-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.5","title":"Logging + audit trail for allow-once","description":"Log short-code issuance, allow-once grants, and expiry actions in a structured way.","notes":"Scope:
- Log fields: code, full hash, command, cwd, timestamp, expiry, allowlist layer used.
- Provide redaction for sensitive arguments.
- Log when a pending code is resolved or expires.
- Ensure log format is compatible with existing logging config.

Acceptance criteria:
- Logs are machine-readable and stable.
- No sensitive data is leaked when redaction is enabled.

Verification:
- Add unit tests for log field presence + redaction behavior; e2e harness must capture issuance/grant/expiry events in verbose logs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.764999152-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T17:02:47.474957587-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.766064669-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:26.60698468-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.6","title":"Unit tests: allow-once correctness","description":"Comprehensive unit tests for hashing, storage, scoping, and expiry.","notes":"Coverage checklist:
- Hash derivation uses exact input tuple (datetime|cwd|command) and optional HMAC secret if enabled.
- 4-char suffix selection is correct and lowercase.
- Collisions: multiple pending entries for same suffix resolve via disambiguation.
- Expiry: entries expire at 24h and are pruned.
- Scope enforcement: cwd mismatch does not allow.
- Redaction: default displays are redacted; `--show-raw` required to reveal full command.
- Default semantics: reusable-until-expiry is allowed on repeated runs.
- Single-use: `--single-use` consumes the entry; subsequent runs block.
- Precedence: explicit user-config blocklist is NOT overridden by allow-once unless `--force` is used; tests cover both paths.
- JSON output includes expected fields.
- Management commands (list/clear/revoke) behave deterministically.

Requirement: tests must use deterministic time (time mocking or injected clock) and reuse common test helpers where possible.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.797304112-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T23:50:43.488803648-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T23:50:43.488803648-05:00","close_reason":"Completed (hashing, scoping, expiry, disambiguation, precedence tests)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.798478354-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:31.653296368-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:36.699380107-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:41.742952515-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:46.787142707-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:41:20.48293591-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.7","title":"E2E tests: allow-once flow","description":"End-to-end tests that exercise the full block → code → allow-once → allow path.","notes":"Scenarios:
- Blocked command emits top-line code.
- `dcg allow-once CODE` allows the exact same command within 24h (repeatable until expiry).
- Same command outside cwd is still blocked.
- After expiry, command is blocked again.
- `--single-use` consumes the exception after first allow.
- Collision handling with two pending entries.
- Explicit user-config blocklist is not overridden; `--force` path is required and is covered in tests.
- `dcg allow-once list` shows pending + active entries (redacted by default).
- `dcg allow-once revoke` removes an active exception.

Logging:
- E2E logs must capture the top-line code, allowlist entry, management actions, and final decision.
- Prefer running via shared e2e harness to keep logs diffable.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.83328164-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T02:46:17.337784702-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.834563285-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:51.831183807-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:36:56.873707039-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:37:01.920769892-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:37:06.965363263-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:41:25.530168568-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.8","title":"Documentation: allow-once usage + safety caveats","description":"Document the allow-once flow and the safety model for users.","notes":"Include:
- Example block output with code at top.
- The exact CLI command to allow.
- Expiry behavior and scope limitations.
- Default semantics: reusable until expiry; `--single-use` for one-shot.
- Redaction defaults and how to use --show-raw safely.
- Precedence: allow-once does not override explicit user-config blocklist unless --force.
- How to remove or list temporary exceptions.

Verification:
- Add a doc consistency check (unit test or CI step) that validates allow-once examples against actual CLI output; record outputs in logs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.86544328-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T17:02:52.508896555-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.8","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.866749661-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.9","title":"Allow-once management commands (list/clear/revoke)","description":"Add CLI subcommands to list and revoke pending or active allow-once exceptions.","notes":"Scope:
- `dcg allow-once list` to show pending codes and active temporary allowlist entries (redacted by default).
- `dcg allow-once clear` to remove expired entries and optionally wipe all pending codes.
- `dcg allow-once revoke <code|hash>` to remove a specific temporary exception.
- All commands support --json for automation.
- Destructive actions (clear/revoke) should require confirmation unless --yes.
- Provide `--show-raw` to display full commands only on explicit request.

Acceptance criteria:
- Users can inspect and revoke temporary exceptions easily without leaking secrets by default.
- Actions are logged and respect redaction settings.

Verification:
- Add unit tests for list/clear/revoke outputs and redaction; e2e harness validates management commands with verbose logging.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:34:15.611602853-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T23:00:45.571974636-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T23:00:45.571974636-05:00","close_reason":"All allow-once management commands (list/clear/revoke) working correctly, all tests pass","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:34:15.613001728-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:37:12.009047051-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:37:17.055358158-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-oien.2","title":"False-positive mitigation strategies (non-exception)","description":"Complementary approaches that reduce false positives without exceptions.","notes":"Goal: reduce noisy blocks by improving classification and policy precision.

Candidates:
1) Safe-string registry expansion (more commands/flags that are data-only).
2) Confidence tiering (warn-only for ambiguous patterns, deny for high-confidence).
3) Explain-first UX (one-line suggestion to run dcg explain to show match details).
4) Better heredoc trigger filtering to reduce accidental scans.

Acceptance criteria:
- Each mitigation is fully specified with tests and explicit safety impact analysis.
- E2E regression harness demonstrates reduced false positives with no unsafe allows.
- Detailed logging captures any warn-only decisions for audit.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.899497505-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T16:34:05.519241259-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:31:36.900660597-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-oien.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:37:27.146680494-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-vfbd","title":"[PACK] email.postmark - Postmark Pack","description":"# Postmark Email Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Postmark API operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### API Operations
- Server deletion: `DELETE /servers/{id}`
- Template deletion: `DELETE /templates/{id}`
- Domain deletion/removal
- Sender signature deletion
- Webhook deletion

## Safe Patterns
- `GET /servers`
- `GET /templates`
- `GET /stats/outbound`
- Template listing
- Stats retrieval
- Email sending (POST)

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: email.* (git_safety_guard-62v)
- Keywords: postmark
- Note: Known for transactional email reliability

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for server deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for template deletion
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/email_postmark.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Server deletion blocked
- [ ] Template deletion blocked
- [ ] Query operations allowed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:25.131023095-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:25.131023095-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vfbd","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:40.276951799-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vfbd","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:40.322325711-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vfbd","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:40.366180575-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vfbd","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:40.407983448-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vfbd","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:40.452461283-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vfbd","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:40.496527034-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vfbd","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:12.021707339-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vfbd","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:12.070220409-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","title":"[PACK] featureflags.launchdarkly - LaunchDarkly Pack","description":"# LaunchDarkly Feature Flags Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for LaunchDarkly CLI operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### ldcli
- `ldcli flags delete`
- `ldcli flags archive` (soft delete)
- `ldcli projects delete`
- `ldcli environments delete`
- `ldcli segments delete`
- `ldcli metrics delete`

### API Operations
- `curl -X DELETE` to app.launchdarkly.com/api

## Safe Patterns
- `ldcli flags list`
- `ldcli flags get`
- `ldcli projects list`
- `ldcli environments list`
- GET API requests

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: featureflags.* (git_safety_guard-ekc)
- Keywords: ldcli, launchdarkly
- Note: Flag archive is soft delete but still risky

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for flag deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for project/environment deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for API patterns
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/featureflags_launchdarkly.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Flag deletion blocked
- [ ] Project deletion blocked
- [ ] List/get operations allowed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:11:42.8329088-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T15:11:42.8329088-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.181287496-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.224883022-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.267010766-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.312213085-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.356939047-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:42.401171782-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:12.683734031-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-vw4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:12.7315613-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-wfr","title":"[PACK] payment.stripe - Stripe Pack","description":"# Stripe Payment Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Stripe CLI and API operations.

- `stripe products delete`
- `stripe prices delete`
- `stripe coupons delete`
- `stripe api_keys roll` (key rotation without coordination)

### API Operations
- `curl -X DELETE api.stripe.com/v1/`

## Safe Patterns
- `stripe listen` (webhook testing)
- `stripe customers list`
- `stripe products list`
- `stripe payments list`
- `stripe logs tail`
- API GET requests

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: payment.* (git_safety_guard-atw)
- Keywords: stripe
- Note: Webhook deletion breaks payment notifications

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Customer deletion blocked
- [ ] Product deletion blocked
- [ ] Key rotation flagged

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-woe","title":"Feature: Memory Leak Detection Tests","description":"# Feature: Memory Leak Detection Tests

## Background

DCG runs as a hook on EVERY Bash command executed by Claude Code. In a typical coding session:
- Hundreds to thousands of commands are executed
- Each command invokes DCG
- Memory leaks accumulate across invocations
- Long sessions could exhaust system memory

CASS has dedicated memory leak tests that run in CI to catch leaks early. DCG needs the same.

## Why This Matters for DCG Specifically

### Usage Pattern
```
User starts Claude Code session
  → Claude executes: git status        → DCG invoked
  → Claude executes: ls -la            → DCG invoked
  → Claude executes: cargo build       → DCG invoked
  → ... (repeat 500+ times)            → DCG invoked each time
Session ends after hours
```

Even small leaks (a few KB per invocation) compound:
- 1000 invocations × 1KB leak = 1MB leaked
- Long session with 5000 invocations = 5MB leaked
- Multiple concurrent sessions = problem

### Critical Code Paths

These DCG components process every command and must not leak:

1. **Input parsing** (`hook.rs`)
   - JSON parsing with serde_json
   - String allocation for command extraction

2. **Pattern matching** (`evaluator.rs`)
   - Regex compilation (lazy_static, but still)
   - Match result allocation

3. **Heredoc processing** (`heredoc.rs`)
   - Content extraction
   - Script parsing
   - AST construction

4. **Output generation** (`trace.rs`, `hook.rs`)
   - JSON serialization
   - Colorful warning formatting

## Technical Approach

### Test Infrastructure

Create `tests/memory_tests.rs` with:

```rust
//! Memory leak detection tests
//! 
//! These tests process many inputs and check that memory usage
//! doesn't grow unboundedly. They must run with --test-threads=1
//! to get accurate measurements.

use std::alloc::{GlobalAlloc, Layout, System};
use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicUsize, Ordering};

/// Custom allocator that tracks allocations
struct TrackingAllocator;

static ALLOCATED: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);

unsafe impl GlobalAlloc for TrackingAllocator {
    unsafe fn alloc(&self, layout: Layout) -> *mut u8 {
        ALLOCATED.fetch_add(layout.size(), Ordering::SeqCst);
        System.alloc(layout)
    }
    
    unsafe fn dealloc(&self, ptr: *mut u8, layout: Layout) {
        ALLOCATED.fetch_sub(layout.size(), Ordering::SeqCst);
        System.dealloc(ptr, layout)
    }
}

fn memory_usage() -> usize {
    ALLOCATED.load(Ordering::SeqCst)
}
```

### Test Cases

1. **Hook input parsing** - Parse 1000 JSON inputs, check memory stable
2. **Pattern evaluation** - Evaluate 1000 commands, check memory stable
3. **Heredoc extraction** - Process 1000 heredocs, check memory stable
4. **Full pipeline** - End-to-end 1000 times, check memory stable

### Memory Budget

Tests should assert:
- Memory growth < 1MB over 1000 iterations
- No single iteration leaks > 10KB
- Final memory within 10% of baseline after GC

## Implementation Tasks

1. **Create memory test infrastructure** - Tracking allocator, helpers
2. **Add hook parsing memory test** - JSON input processing
3. **Add pattern matching memory test** - Evaluator path
4. **Add heredoc processing memory test** - Script extraction
5. **Add memory tests to CI workflow** - Dedicated job

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Memory tests exist and pass locally
- [ ] Memory tests run in CI (dedicated job)
- [ ] Tests catch intentionally introduced leaks
- [ ] Memory growth < 1MB over 1000 iterations
- [ ] Tests run with --test-threads=1 for accuracy

## Considerations

### Test Isolation
Memory tests must run sequentially (--test-threads=1) to get accurate measurements. Other tests running concurrently would pollute measurements.

### CI Resources
Memory tests should run on a dedicated CI job to:
- Avoid interference with other tests
- Have predictable resource allocation
- Allow longer timeouts if needed

### Platform Differences
Memory tracking may behave differently on Linux vs macOS. Focus on Linux (CI environment) but tests should pass everywhere.

### False Positives
Some "leaks" are actually intentional caches:
- Lazy static regex patterns (compile once, keep forever)
- Pack pattern registry (loaded once at startup)

Tests should account for expected one-time allocations.

## References

- CASS memory tests: /data/projects/coding_agent_session_search/.github/workflows/bench.yml (memory-tests job)
- Rust tracking allocator: https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/alloc/trait.GlobalAlloc.html","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:21:21.710163301-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T14:21:21.710163301-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-woe","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-dj8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:25:12.375811198-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-woe","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-39k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:25:12.406752934-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-woe","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-8zo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:25:12.43467007-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"git_safety_guard-woe","title":"Feature: Memory Leak Detection Tests","description":"# Feature: Memory Leak Detection Tests

## Background

DCG runs as a hook on EVERY Bash command executed by Claude Code. In a typical coding session:
- Hundreds to thousands of commands are executed
- Each command invokes DCG
- Memory leaks accumulate across invocations
- Long sessions could exhaust system memory

CASS has dedicated memory leak tests that run in CI to catch leaks early. DCG needs the same.

## Why This Matters for DCG Specifically

### Usage Pattern
```
User starts Claude Code session
  → Claude executes: git status        → DCG invoked
  → Claude executes: ls -la            → DCG invoked
  → Claude executes: cargo build       → DCG invoked
  → ... (repeat 500+ times)            → DCG invoked each time
Session ends after hours
```

Even small leaks (a few KB per invocation) compound:
- 1000 invocations × 1KB leak = 1MB leaked
- Long session with 5000 invocations = 5MB leaked
- Multiple concurrent sessions = problem

### Critical Code Paths

These DCG components process every command and must not leak:

1. **Input parsing** (`hook.rs`)
   - JSON parsing with serde_json
   - String allocation for command extraction

2. **Pattern matching** (`evaluator.rs`)
   - Regex compilation (lazy_static, but still)
   - Match result allocation

3. **Heredoc processing** (`heredoc.rs`)
   - Content extraction
   - Script parsing
   - AST construction

4. **Output generation** (`trace.rs`, `hook.rs`)
   - JSON serialization
   - Colorful warning formatting

## Technical Approach

### Test Infrastructure

Create `tests/memory_tests.rs` with:

```rust
//! Memory leak detection tests
//! 
//! These tests process many inputs and check that memory usage
//! doesn't grow unboundedly. They must run with --test-threads=1
//! to get accurate measurements.

use std::alloc::{GlobalAlloc, Layout, System};
use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicUsize, Ordering};

/// Custom allocator that tracks allocations
struct TrackingAllocator;

static ALLOCATED: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);

unsafe impl GlobalAlloc for TrackingAllocator {
    unsafe fn alloc(&self, layout: Layout) -> *mut u8 {
        ALLOCATED.fetch_add(layout.size(), Ordering::SeqCst);
        System.alloc(layout)
    }
    
    unsafe fn dealloc(&self, ptr: *mut u8, layout: Layout) {
        ALLOCATED.fetch_sub(layout.size(), Ordering::SeqCst);
        System.dealloc(ptr, layout)
    }
}

fn memory_usage() -> usize {
    ALLOCATED.load(Ordering::SeqCst)
}
```

### Test Cases

1. **Hook input parsing** - Parse 1000 JSON inputs, check memory stable
2. **Pattern evaluation** - Evaluate 1000 commands, check memory stable
3. **Heredoc extraction** - Process 1000 heredocs, check memory stable
4. **Full pipeline** - End-to-end 1000 times, check memory stable

### Memory Budget

Tests should assert:
- Memory growth < 1MB over 1000 iterations
- No single iteration leaks > 10KB
- Final memory within 10% of baseline after GC

## Implementation Tasks

1. **Create memory test infrastructure** - Tracking allocator, helpers
2. **Add hook parsing memory test** - JSON input processing
3. **Add pattern matching memory test** - Evaluator path
4. **Add heredoc processing memory test** - Script extraction
5. **Add memory tests to CI workflow** - Dedicated job

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Memory tests exist and pass locally
- [ ] Memory tests run in CI (dedicated job)
- [ ] Tests catch intentionally introduced leaks
- [ ] Memory growth < 1MB over 1000 iterations
- [ ] Tests run with --test-threads=1 for accuracy

## Considerations

### Test Isolation
Memory tests must run sequentially (--test-threads=1) to get accurate measurements. Other tests running concurrently would pollute measurements.

### CI Resources
Memory tests should run on a dedicated CI job to:
- Avoid interference with other tests
- Have predictable resource allocation
- Allow longer timeouts if needed

### Platform Differences
Memory tracking may behave differently on Linux vs macOS. Focus on Linux (CI environment) but tests should pass everywhere.

### False Positives
Some "leaks" are actually intentional caches:
- Lazy static regex patterns (compile once, keep forever)
- Pack pattern registry (loaded once at startup)

Tests should account for expected one-time allocations.

## References

- CASS memory tests: /data/projects/coding_agent_session_search/.github/workflows/bench.yml (memory-tests job)

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-wso","title":"[CATEGORY] cdn.* - CDN & Edge Pack Suite","description":"# CDN & Edge Pack Suite

## Overview
CDN and edge services cache content globally. Cache purges and config changes affect all users. Origin configuration changes can cause global outages.

## Packs to Implement

### cdn.cloudflare
- **Why Critical**: Cloudflare provides CDN, security, and edge compute. Rule deletion or purge affects global traffic.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `wrangler delete` - Deletes Workers
  - `wrangler kv:key delete` / `kv:namespace delete`
  - Cache purge operations
  - Firewall rule deletion
  - Page rule deletion
- **Safe Patterns**: `wrangler whoami`, `list`, `get`

### cdn.fastly
- **Why Critical**: Fastly powers high-traffic sites. Service version activation can cause outages.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `fastly service delete`
  - `fastly domain delete`
  - `fastly backend delete`
  - VCL changes via API
- **Safe Patterns**: `describe`, `list`, `version list`

### cdn.cloudfront
- **Why Critical**: AWS CloudFront is deeply integrated with AWS services.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `aws cloudfront delete-distribution`
  - `aws cloudfront create-invalidation` (cache purge - costly)
  - Origin configuration changes
- **Safe Patterns**: `list-`, `get-`, `describe-`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 3 Epic (git_safety_guard-9ic)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Major CDN providers covered
- [ ] Cache purge operations flagged
- [ ] Edge compute deletion detected
- [ ] Configuration changes handled","notes":"Testing/Validation:
- Every cdn.* pack must comply with Standard Pack Task Requirements (git_safety_guard-ikf2), including unit tests and e2e corpus scenarios.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-wy72","title":"[PACK] storage.gcs - Google Cloud Storage Pack","description":"# Google Cloud Storage Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for GCS operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### gsutil
- `gsutil rb` - Remove bucket
- `gsutil rb -f` - Force remove bucket
- `gsutil -m rm` - Multi-threaded remove
- Recursive delete operations

### gcloud storage
- `gcloud storage buckets delete`
- `gcloud storage objects delete`
- `gcloud storage rm`

## Safe Patterns
- `gsutil ls`
- `gsutil cat`
- `gsutil stat`
- `gsutil du`
- `gsutil cp` (copy/download)
- `gcloud storage buckets list`
- `gcloud storage objects list`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: storage.* (git_safety_guard-skt)
- Keywords: gsutil, gcloud
- Note: Both gsutil and gcloud storage commands

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for bucket removal
- [ ] Unit tests for object deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for recursive operations
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/storage_gcs.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bucket removal blocked
- [ ] Object deletion blocked
- [ ] List/copy operations allowed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:26.208275911-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:26.208275911-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-wy72","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:40.014879026-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-wy72","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:40.057245539-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-wy72","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:40.101959859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-wy72","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:40.14754028-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-wy72","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:40.190634442-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-wy72","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:40.234314787-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-wy72","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.927048337-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-wy72","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:11.975729413-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-x3g","title":"[CATEGORY] dns.* - DNS Management Pack Suite","description":"# DNS Management Pack Suite

## Overview
DNS is critical infrastructure. Incorrect DNS changes can make services unreachable. Zone deletion or record manipulation affects all users globally.

## Packs to Implement

### dns.cloudflare
- **Why Critical**: Cloudflare manages DNS for millions of domains. Zone/record deletion causes immediate outages.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `cloudflare dns delete` (wrangler)
  - `curl -X DELETE api.cloudflare.com/.../dns_records/`
  - `terraform destroy` on cloudflare resources
- **Safe Patterns**: `list`, `show`, GET requests

### dns.route53
- **Why Critical**: AWS Route53 handles DNS for AWS-hosted services. Hosted zone deletion affects all records.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `aws route53 delete-hosted-zone`
  - `aws route53 change-resource-record-sets` with DELETE action
  - `aws route53 delete-health-check`
- **Safe Patterns**: `list-`, `get-`, `test-dns-answer`

### dns.generic
- **Why Critical**: Generic DNS tools (dig, host, nslookup, nsupdate) - nsupdate can modify records.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `nsupdate` with delete commands
  - Zone transfers to untrusted servers
- **Safe Patterns**: `dig`, `host`, `nslookup` (read-only)

## Rationale
DNS changes propagate globally with TTL delays. A bad change can take hours to fix even after correction. Zone delegation changes can effectively transfer domain control.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 2 Epic (git_safety_guard-hhh)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Major DNS providers covered
- [ ] API-based changes detected
- [ ] Record type changes handled appropriately
- [ ] Tests verify propagation-sensitive operations flagged","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:28:25.946071182-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:19:37.007084357-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T22:19:37.007084357-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x3g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jt0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:34:31.413213489-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x3g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ef4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:34:31.441389982-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x3g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-t2r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:56.23175429-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x3g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:46.932811111-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x3g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:46.982993279-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x3g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.030792685-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x3g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.077266947-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x3g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.122525902-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-x3g","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.168603157-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
